declarations.name: getAssetPath
declarations.description: Get a resolved path from where an asset can be loaded.

parameters.name: suffix
parameters.description: The relative path for the asset.

declarations.name: setAssetPath
declarations.description:
Used to manually set the base path where package assets (like localization
and icons) can be found.

By default, the package assets are loaded from
`https://js.arcgis.com/<version>/<simplified-package-name>/`. We are hosting
our assets on a CDN (Content Delivery Network) to ensure fast and reliable
access. It is CORS-enabled, so you can load the assets from any domain. This
is the recommended way to load the assets and avoid bundling them with your
application.

However, if you need to host the assets locally, you can copy them manually
as part of your build process and use `setAssetPath` to customize where the
browser will load the assets from.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Relative or absolute path to the assets folder.

type.text: K extends `aria-${infer Rest}` ? Rest : K extends `aria${infer Rest}` ? Uncapitalize<Rest> : K

members.description:
Specifies the selection mode of the component, where:

declarations.name: Alert
declarations.description:
Alerts are meant to provide a way to communicate urgent or important information to users, frequently as a result of an action they took in your app. Alerts are positioned
at the bottom of the page. Multiple opened alerts will be added to a queue, allowing users to dismiss them in the order they are provided.

members.name: autoClose
members.description: When `true`, the component closes automatically. Recommended for passive, non-blocking alerts.

members.name: autoCloseDuration
members.description: Specifies the duration before the component automatically closes - only use with `autoClose`.

members.name: icon
members.description:
When `true`, shows a default recommended icon. Alternatively,
pass a Calcite UI Icon name to display a specific icon.

members.name: iconFlipRtl
members.description: When `true`, the icon will be flipped when the element direction is right-to-left (`"rtl"`).

members.name: kind
members.description: Specifies the kind of the component, which will apply to top border and icon.

members.name: label
members.description: Specifies an accessible label for the component.

members.name: messageOverrides
members.description: Overrides individual strings used by the component.

members.name: numberingSystem
members.description: Specifies the Unicode numeral system used by the component for localization.

members.name: open
members.description: When `true`, displays and positions the component.

members.name: placement
members.description: Specifies the placement of the component.

members.name: queue
members.description: Specifies the ordering priority of the component when opened.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: topLayerDisabled
members.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

members.description: Create a promise that resolves once component is fully loaded.

attributes.name: overlay-positioning
attributes.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

attributes.name: top-layer-disabled
attributes.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

To drag elements from one group into another, both groups must have the same group value.

declarations.name: Button
declarations.description:
Passing a 'href' will render an anchor link, instead of a button. Role will be set to link, or button, depending on this.

It is the consumers responsibility to add aria information, rel, target, for links, and any button attributes for form submission

members.name: allowCustomValues
members.description: When `true`, allows entry of custom values, which are not in the original set of items.

members.name: clearDisabled
members.description: When `true`, disables value-clearing.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents interaction and decreases the component's opacity.

members.name: filteredItems
members.description: The component's filtered items.

members.name: filterProps
members.description: Specifies the properties to match against when filtering. If not set, all properties will be matched (`description`, `label`, `metadata`, `shortHeading`).

members.name: filterText
members.description: The component's filter input field text.

members.name: flipPlacements
members.description: Specifies the component's fallback `placement` for slotted content when it's initial or specified `placement` has insufficient space available.

members.name: form
members.description:
Specifies the `id` of the component's associated form.

When not set, the component is associated with its ancestor form element, if one exists.

members.name: label
members.description: Specifies an accessible label for the component.

members.name: labelText
members.description: Specifies the component's label text.

members.name: maxItems
members.description: Specifies the maximum number of `calcite-combobox-item`s (including nested children) to display before displaying a scrollbar.

members.name: messageOverrides
members.description: Overrides individual strings used by the component.

members.name: name
members.description: Specifies the name of the component. Required to pass the component's `value` on form submission.

members.name: open
members.description: When `true`, displays and positions the component.

members.name: overlayPositioning
members.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Specifies the input's placeholder text.

members.name: placeholderIcon
members.description: Specifies the input's placeholder icon.

members.name: placeholderIconFlipRtl
members.description: When `true` and the element direction is right-to-left (`"rtl"`), flips the input's `placeholderIcon`.

members.name: placement
members.description: Specifies the component's position relative to the `referenceElement`.

members.name: readOnly
members.description: When `true`, the component's value can be read, but controls are not accessible and the value cannot be modified.

members.name: required
members.description:
When `true` and the component resides in a form,
the component must have a value in order for the form to submit.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: selectAllEnabled
members.description: When `true` and `selectionMode` is `"multiple"` or `"ancestors"`, displays a checkbox for selecting all `calcite-combobox-item`s.

members.name: selectedItems
members.description: The component's selected items.

members.name: selectionDisplay
members.description:
When `selectionMode` is `"ancestors"` or `"multiple"`, specifies the display of multiple `calcite-combobox-item` selections, where:

`"all"` displays all selections with individual `calcite-chip`s,

`"fit"` displays individual `calcite-chip`s that scale to the component's size, including a non-closable `calcite-chip`, which provides the number of additional `calcite-combobox-item` selections not visually displayed, and

`"single"` displays one `calcite-chip` with the total number of selections.

members.name: selectionMode
members.description:
Specifies the selection mode of the component, where:

`"multiple"` allows any number of selections,

`"single"` allows only one selection,

`"single-persist"` allows one selection and prevents de-selection, and

`"ancestors"` allows multiple selections, but shows ancestors of selected items as selected, with only deepest children shown in chips.

members.name: status
members.description: Specifies the input field's status, which determines message and icons.

members.name: topLayerDisabled
members.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

members.name: validationIcon
members.description: Specifies the validation icon to display under the component.

members.name: validationMessage
members.description: Specifies the validation message to display under the component.

members.name: validity
members.description: The component's current validation state.

members.name: value
members.description: The component's value(s) from the selected `calcite-combobox-item`(s).

members.name: componentOnReady
members.description: Create a promise that resolves once component is fully loaded.

members.description: When `true`, disables the component's close button.

members.description: Specifies the component's description.

members.name: dragEnabled
members.description: When `true`, the component is draggable.

members.name: escapeDisabled
members.description:
When `true`, disables the default close on escape behavior.

By default, an open dialog can be dismissed by pressing the Esc key.

members.name: focusTrapDisabled
members.description: When `true` and `modal` is `false`, prevents focus trapping.

members.name: focusTrapOptions
members.description:
Specifies custom focus trap configuration on the component, where

`"allowOutsideClick`" allows outside clicks,
`"initialFocus"` enables initial focus,
`"returnFocusOnDeactivate"` returns focus when not active,
`"extraContainers"` specifies additional focusable elements external to the trap, such as 3rd-party components appending elements to the document body, and
`"setReturnFocus"` customizes the element to which focus is returned when the trap is deactivated. Return `false` to prevent focus return, or `undefined` to use the default behavior (returning focus to the element focused before activation).

members.name: fullscreenDisabled
members.description: When `true`, the component will not display at fullscreen, which may be desired in limited display areas, such as mobile devices.

members.name: heading
members.description: Specifies the component's heading text.

members.name: headingLevel
members.description: Specifies the heading level number of the component's `heading` for proper document structure, without affecting visual styling.

members.name: icon
members.description: Specifies an icon to display.

members.name: iconFlipRtl
members.description: When `true` and the element direction is right-to-left (`"rtl"`), flips the component`s `icon`.

members.name: kind
members.description: Specifies the component's kind, which determines the top border styling.

members.name: loading
members.description: When `true`, a busy indicator is displayed.

members.name: menuOpen
members.description: When `true`, the action menu items in the `header-menu-actions` slot are open.

members.name: messageOverrides
members.description: Overrides individual strings used by the component.

members.name: modal
members.description: When `true`, displays a scrim blocking interaction underneath the component.

members.name: open
members.description: When `true`, displays and positions the component.

members.name: outsideCloseDisabled
members.description: When `true`, disables the closing of the component when clicked outside.

members.name: overlayPositioning
members.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

members.name: placement
members.description: Specifies the component's placement.

members.name: resizable
members.description: When `true`, the component is resizable.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: topLayerDisabled
members.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

members.name: width
members.description: Specifies the component's width.

members.deprecated: in v3.0.0, removal target v6.0.0 - Use the `width` property instead.
members.description: Specifies the component's width.

members.name: updateSize
members.description:
Updates the component's size by setting its inline and/or block dimensions.

Use this method to programmatically override the components's width (inline) and/or height (block).
Pass `null` to clear the override and revert to the default or CSS variable size.

slots.name: footer
slots.description: A slot for adding custom content to the component's footer. Should not be used with the `footer-start` or `footer-end` slots.

members.name: closeOnSelectDisabled
members.description:
When `true`, the component will remain open after a selection is made.

If the `selectionMode` of the selected `calcite-dropdown-item`'s containing `calcite-dropdown-group` is `"none"`, the component will always close.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents interaction and decreases the component's opacity.

members.name: flipPlacements
members.description: Specifies the component's fallback `placement` for slotted `calcite-dropdown-item`s when their initial or specified `placement` has insufficient space available.

members.name: maxItems
members.description:
Specifies the maximum number of `calcite-dropdown-item`s to display before showing a scrollbar.
Value must be greater than `0`, and does not include `groupTitle`s from `calcite-dropdown-group`.

members.name: offsetDistance
members.description: Specifies the distance to position the component away from the `referenceElement`.

members.name: offsetSkidding
members.description: Specifies the distance to position the component along the `referenceElement`.

members.name: open
members.description: When `true`, displays and positions the component.

members.name: overlayPositioning
members.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

members.name: placement
members.description: Determines the component's placement relative to the container element.

members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: topLayerDisabled
members.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

members.name: accept

When `type` is `"file"`, specifies a comma separated list of unique file type specifiers for limiting accepted file types.
Read the native attribute's documentation on MDN for more info.

members.name: autocomplete
members.description:
Specifies the type of content to autocomplete, for use in forms.
Read the native attribute's documentation on MDN for more info.

members.name: clearable
members.description: When `true` and the component has a `value`, a clear button is displayed. The clear button shows by default for `"search"`, `"time"`, and `"date"` types.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents interaction and decreases the component's opacity.

members.name: files
members.description: When `type` is `"file"`, specifies the component's selected files.

members.name: form
members.description:
Specifies the `id` of the component's associated form.

When not set, the component is associated with its ancestor form element, if one exists.

members.name: groupSeparator
members.description: When `true`, number values are displayed with a group separator corresponding to the language and country format.

members.name: icon
members.description: When `true`, displays a default recommended icon. Alternatively, pass a Calcite UI Icon name to display a specific icon.

members.name: iconFlipRtl
members.description: When `true` and the element direction is right-to-left (`"rtl"`), flips the component`s `icon`.

members.name: label
members.description: Specifies an accessible label for the component.

members.name: labelText
members.description: Specifies the component's label text.

members.name: loading
members.description: When `true`, a busy indicator is displayed.

members.name: max
members.description:
When the component resides in a form,
specifies the maximum value for `type="number"`.

members.name: maxLength
members.description:
When the component resides in a form,
specifies the maximum length of text for the component's `value`.

members.name: messageOverrides
members.description: Overrides individual strings used by the component.

members.name: min
members.description:
When the component resides in a form,
specifies the minimum value for `type="number"`.

members.name: minLength
members.description:
When the component resides in a form,
specifies the minimum length of text for the component's `value`.

members.name: multiple
members.description:
When `true` and `type` is `"email"` or `"file"`, the component can accept more than one value.
Read the native attribute's documentation on MDN for more info.

members.name: name
members.description:
Specifies the name of the component.

Required to pass the component's `value` on form submission.

members.name: numberButtonType
members.description: When `type="number"`, specifies the placement of the buttons.

members.name: numberingSystem
members.description: Specifies the Unicode numeral system used by the component for localization.

members.name: pattern
members.description:
When the component resides in a form,
specifies a regular expression (regex) pattern the component's `value` must match for validation.
Read the native attribute's documentation on MDN for more info.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Specifies the component's placeholder text.

members.name: prefixText
members.description: Specifies text to display at the start of the component.

members.name: readOnly
members.description: When `true`, the component's `value` can be read, but cannot be modified.

members.name: required
members.description:
When `true` and the component resides in a form,
the component must have a `value` in order for the form to submit.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: status
members.description: Specifies the input field's status, which determines message and icons.

members.name: step
members.description: Specifies the granularity the component's `value` must adhere to.

members.name: suffixText
members.description: Specifies text to display at the end of the component.

members.name: type
members.description:
Specifies the component type.

Note that the following `type`s add type-specific icons by default: `"date"`, `"email"`, `"password"`, `"search"`, `"tel"`, `"time"`.

members.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

attributes.description: When `range` is `true`, specifies the number of calendars displayed.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: When `true`, prevents interaction and decreases the component's opacity.

attributes.name: focus-trap-disabled
attributes.description: When `true`, prevents focus trapping.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
Specifies the `id` of the component's associated form.

When not set, the component is associated with its ancestor form element, if one exists.

attributes.name: heading-level
attributes.description: Specifies the heading level number of the component's `heading` for proper document structure, without affecting visual styling.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Specifies an accessible label for the component.

attributes.name: label-text
attributes.description: Specifies the component's label text.

attributes.name: layout
attributes.description: Defines the component's layout.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description:
When the component resides in a form,
specifies the latest allowed date ("yyyy-mm-dd").

attributes.name: min
attributes.description:
When the component resides in a form,
specifies the earliest allowed date ("yyyy-mm-dd").

attributes.name: month-style
attributes.description: Specifies the component's month style.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Specifies the name of the component. Required to pass the component's `value` on form submission.

attributes.name: numbering-system
attributes.description: Specifies the Unicode numeral system used by the component for localization. This property cannot be dynamically changed.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: When `true`, displays the `calcite-date-picker` component.

attributes.name: overlay-positioning
attributes.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: Determines the `calcite-date-picker`'s placement relative to the input.

attributes.name: proximity-selection-disabled
attributes.description:
When `true`, disables the default behavior on the third click of narrowing or extending the range.
Instead starts a new range.

attributes.name: range
attributes.description: When `true`, activates a range for the component.

attributes.name: read-only
attributes.description: When `true`, the component's `value` can be read, but controls are not accessible and the `value` cannot be modified.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description:
When `true` and the component resides in a form,
the component must have a `value` in order for the form to submit.

attributes.name: scale
attributes.description: Specifies the size of the component.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Specifies the input field's status, which determines message and icons.

attributes.name: top-layer-disabled
attributes.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

attributes.name: validation-icon
attributes.description: Specifies the validation icon to display under the component.

attributes.name: validation-message
attributes.description: Specifies the validation message to display under the component.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Selected date as a string in ISO format (`"yyyy-mm-dd"`).

events.name: calciteInputDatePickerBeforeClose
events.description: Fires when the component is requested to be closed and before the closing transition begins.

events.name: calciteInputDatePickerBeforeOpen
events.description: Fires when the component is added to the DOM but not rendered, and before the opening transition begins.

events.name: calciteInputDatePickerChange
events.description: Fires when the component's `value` changes.

events.name: calciteInputDatePickerClose
events.description: Fires when the component is closed and animation is complete.

events.name: calciteInputDatePickerOpen
events.description: Fires when the component is opened and animation is complete.

slots.name: label-content
slots.description: A slot for rendering content next to the component's `labelText`.

members.name: clearable
members.description:
When `true`, an empty value (`null`) will be allowed as a `value`.

When `false`, an offset or name value is enforced, and clearing the input or blurring will restore the last valid `value`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents interaction and decreases the component's opacity.

members.name: form
members.description:
Specifies the `id` of the component's associated form.

When not set, the component is associated with its ancestor form element, if one exists.

members.name: labelText
members.description: Specifies the component's label text.

members.name: maxItems
members.description: Specifies the component's maximum number of options to display before displaying a scrollbar.

members.name: messageOverrides
members.description: Overrides individual strings used by the component.

members.name: mode
members.description:
This specifies the type of `value` and the associated options presented to the user:

Using `"offset"` will provide options that show timezone offsets.

Using `"name"` will provide options that show the IANA time zone names.

members.name: name
members.description: Specifies the name of the component. Required to pass the component's `value` on form submission.

members.name: offsetStyle
members.description:
When `mode` is `"offset"`, specifies how the offset will be displayed, where

`"user"` uses `UTC` or `GMT` depending on the user's locale,
`"gmt"` always uses `GMT`, and
`"utc"` always uses `UTC`.

members.name: open
members.description: When `true`, displays and positions the component.

members.name: overlayPositioning
members.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

members.name: readOnly
members.description: When `true`, the component's `value` can be read, but controls are not accessible and the `value` cannot be modified.

members.name: referenceDate
members.description:
This `date` will be used as a reference to Daylight Savings Time when creating time zone item groups.

It can be either a Date instance or a string in ISO format (`"YYYY-MM-DD"`, `"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ"`).

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: status
members.description: Specifies the input field's status, which determines message and icons.

members.name: topLayerDisabled
members.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

members.name: validationIcon
members.description: Specifies the validation icon to display under the component.

members.name: validationMessage
members.description: Specifies the validation message to display under the component.

members.name: validity
members.description: The component's current validation state.

members.name: value
members.description:
The component's value, where the value is the time zone offset or the difference, in minutes, between the selected time zone and UTC.

If no value is provided, the user's time zone offset will be selected by default.

members.name: componentOnReady
members.description: Create a promise that resolves once component is fully loaded.

declarations.name: Link
declarations.description:
Any attributes placed on <calcite-link> component will propagate to the rendered child

Passing a 'href' will render an anchor link, instead of a button.

It is the consumers responsibility to add aria information, rel, target, for links, and any link attributes for form submission

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents interaction and decreases the component's opacity.

members.name: download
members.description:
When specified, prompts the user to save the linked URL instead of navigating to it. Can be used with or without a value:
Without a value, the browser will suggest a filename/extension.

members.name: href
members.description: Specifies the URL of the linked resource, which can be set as an absolute or relative path.

members.name: iconEnd
members.description: Specifies an icon to display at the end of the component.

members.name: iconFlipRtl
members.description: When `true` and the element direction is right-to-left (`"rtl"`), flips the component's `iconStart` and/or `iconEnd`.

members.name: iconStart
members.description: Specifies an icon to display at the start of the component.

members.name: rel
members.description: Specifies the relationship to the linked resource defined in `href`.

members.name: target
members.description: Specifies the frame or window to open the linked resource.

declarations.name: List
declarations.description: A general purpose list that enables users to construct list items that conform to Calcite styling.

members.name: canPull
members.description: When provided, the method will be called to determine whether the element can move from the list.

members.name: canPut
members.description: When provided, the method will be called to determine whether the element can be added from another list.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, interaction is prevented and the component is displayed with lower opacity.

members.name: displayMode
members.description:
Specifies the nesting behavior of `calcite-list-item`s, where

`"flat"` displays `calcite-list-item`s in a uniform list, and

`"nested"` displays `calcite-list-item`s under their parent element.

 The parent component's behavior should follow throughout its child elements.

members.name: dragEnabled
members.description: When `true`, `calcite-list-item`s are sortable via a draggable button.

members.name: filteredData
members.description: The currently filtered `calcite-list-item` data.

members.name: filteredItems
members.description: The currently filtered `calcite-list-item`s.

members.name: filterEnabled
members.description: When `true`, an input appears at the top of the component that can be used by end users to filter `calcite-list-item`s.

members.name: filterLabel
members.description: Specifies an accessible name for the filter input field.

members.name: filterPlaceholder
members.description: Specifies placeholder text for the component's filter input field.

members.name: filterPredicate
members.description: Specifies a function to handle filtering.

docsTags.name: example

members.name: filterProps
members.description: Specifies the properties to match against when filtering. If not set, all properties will be matched (`description`, `label`, `metadata`, and the `calcite-list-item-group`'s `heading`).

members.name: filterText
members.description: Text for the component's filter input field.

members.name: group
members.description:
The component's group identifier.

To drag elements from one list into another, both lists must have the same group value.

members.name: interactionMode
members.description:
Specifies the interaction mode of the component, where

`"interactive"` allows interaction styling and pointer changes on hover,

`"static"` does not allow interaction styling and pointer changes on hover -

the `"static"` value should only be used when `selectionMode` is `"none"`.

members.name: label
members.description:
Specifies an accessible label for the component.

When `dragEnabled` is `true` and multiple list sorting is enabled with `group`, specifies the component's name for dragging between lists.

members.name: loading
members.description: When `true`, a busy indicator is displayed.

members.name: messageOverrides
members.description: Overrides individual strings used by the component.

members.name: numberingSystem
members.description: Specifies the Unicode numeral system used by the component for localization.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: selectedItems
members.description: The currently selected items.

members.name: selectionAppearance
members.description:
Specifies the selection appearance, where

`"icon"` displays a checkmark or dot,

`"border"` [Deprecated] - Use `"highlight"` instead - displays a border, or

`"highlight"` displays background highlight.

members.name: selectionMode
members.description:
Specifies the selection mode of the component, where:

`"multiple"` allows any number of selections,

`"single"` allows only one selection,

`"single-persist"` allows one selection and prevents de-selection, and

`"none"` does not allow any selections.

members.name: sortDisabled
members.description: When `true`, and a `group` is defined, `calcite-list-item`s are no longer sortable.

members.description: Create a promise that resolves once component is fully loaded.

`"multiple"` allows any number of selections,

`"single"` allows only one selection,

`"single-persist"` allows one selection and prevents de-selection, and

`"none"` does not allow any selections.

events.description: Fires when the component's item order changes.

members.name: selected

members.name: sortHandleOpen

members.name: topLayerDisabled

members.name: appearance
members.description: Specifies the appearance of the component.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, interaction is prevented and the component is displayed with lower opacity.

members.name: fillType
members.description:
Specifies the component's display, where
`"single"` displays a single color, and
`"range"` displays a range of colors based on provided `low`, `high`, `min` or `max` values.

members.name: form
members.description:
Specifies the `id` of the component's associated form.

When not set, the component is associated with its ancestor form element, if one exists.

members.name: groupSeparator
members.description: When `true`, number values are displayed with a group separator corresponding to the language and country format.

members.name: high
members.description: Specifies a high value.  When `fillType` is `"range"`, displays a different color when above the specified threshold.

members.name: label
members.description: Specifies an accessible label for the component.

members.name: low
members.description: Specifies a low value.  When `fillType` is `"range"`, displays a different color when above the specified threshold.

members.name: max
members.description: Specifies the component's highest allowed value.

members.name: min
members.description: Specifies the component's lowest allowed value.

members.name: name
members.description: Specifies the name of the component. Required to pass the component's `value` on form submission.

members.name: numberingSystem
members.description: Specifies the Unicode numeral system used by the component for localization.

members.name: rangeLabels
members.description: When `true`, displays the values of `high`, `low`, `min`, and `max`.

members.name: rangeLabelType
members.description: When `rangeLabels` is `true`, specifies the format of displayed labels.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: unitLabel
members.description: When `rangeLabelType` is `"units"` and either `valueLabel` or `rangeLabels` are `true`, displays beside the `value` and/or  `min` values.

members.name: value
members.description: Specifies the component's value.

members.name: valueLabel
members.description: When `true`, displays the `value`.

members.name: valueLabelType
members.description: When `valueLabel` is `true`, specifies the format of displayed label.

members.description: Create a promise that resolves once component is fully loaded.

declarations.name: Notice
declarations.description:
Notices are intended to be used to present users with important-but-not-crucial contextual tips or copy. Because
notices are displayed inline, a common use case is displaying them on page-load to present users with short hints or contextual copy.
They are optionally closable - useful for keeping track of whether or not a user has closed the notice. You can also choose not
to display a notice on page load and set the "active" attribute as needed to contextually provide inline messaging to users.

members.name: autoClose
members.description: When `true`, clicking outside of the component automatically closes open `calcite-popover`s.

members.name: closable
members.description: When `true`, displays a close button in the component.

members.name: flipDisabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents flipping the component's placement when overlapping its `referenceElement`.

members.name: flipPlacements
members.description: Specifies the component's fallback `placement` for slotted content when it's initial or specified `placement` has insufficient space available.

members.name: focusTrapDisabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents focus trapping.

members.name: focusTrapOptions
members.description:
Specifies custom focus trap configuration on the component, where

`"allowOutsideClick`" allows outside clicks,
`"initialFocus"` enables initial focus,
`"returnFocusOnDeactivate"` returns focus when not active,
`"extraContainers"` specifies additional focusable elements external to the trap, such as 3rd-party components appending elements to the document body, and
`"setReturnFocus"` customizes the element to which focus is returned when the trap is deactivated. Return `false` to prevent focus return, or `undefined` to use the default behavior (returning focus to the element focused before activation).

members.name: heading
members.description: Specifies the component's heading text.

members.name: headingLevel
members.description: Specifies the heading level number of the component's `heading` for proper document structure, without affecting visual styling.

members.name: label
members.description: Specifies an accessible label for the component.

members.name: messageOverrides
members.description: Overrides individual strings used by the component.

members.name: offsetDistance
members.description: Specifies the distance to position the component away from the `referenceElement`.

members.name: offsetSkidding
members.description: Specifies the distance to position the component along the `referenceElement`.

members.name: open
members.description: When `true`, displays and positions the component.

members.name: overlayPositioning
members.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

members.name: placement
members.description: Determines where the component will be positioned relative to the `referenceElement`.

members.name: pointerDisabled
members.description: When `true`, removes the caret pointer.

members.name: referenceElement
members.description:
The `referenceElement` is used to position the component according to its `placement` value.

Setting the value to an `HTMLElement` is preferred so the component does not need to query the DOM.

However, a string `id` of the reference element can also be used.

The component should not be placed within its own `referenceElement` to avoid unintended behavior.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the size of the component.

members.name: topLayerDisabled
members.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

members.name: triggerDisabled
members.description:
When `true`, disables automatically toggling the component when its `referenceElement` has been triggered.

This property can be set to `true` to manage when the component is open.

members.name: componentOnReady
members.description: Create a promise that resolves once component is fully loaded.

events.name: calciteRadioButtonChange
events.description:
Fires only when the radio button is checked.  This behavior is identical to the native HTML input element.
Since this event does not fire when the radio button is unchecked, it's not recommended to attach a listener for this event
directly on the element, but instead either attach it to a node that contains all of the radio buttons in the group
or use the `calciteRadioButtonGroupChange` event if using this with `calcite-radio-button-group`.

members.name: displayMode
members.description:
Specifies the display mode - `"float"` separates content from main layout,
and `"overlay"` displays on top of center content.

members.name: escapeDisabled
members.description: When `true`, disables the default close on escape behavior.

members.name: focusTrapDisabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents focus trapping.

members.name: focusTrapOptions
members.description:
Specifies custom focus trap configuration on the component, where

`"allowOutsideClick"` allows outside clicks,
`"initialFocus"` enables initial focus,
`"returnFocusOnDeactivate"` returns focus when not active,
`"extraContainers"` specifies additional focusable elements external to the trap, such as 3rd-party components appending elements to the document body, and
`"setReturnFocus"` customizes the element to which focus is returned when the trap is deactivated. Return `false` to prevent focus return, or `undefined` to use the default behavior (returning focus to the element focused before activation).

members.description: Specifies the component's height.

members.name: position
members.description: Determines where the component will be positioned.

members.name: topLayerDisabled
members.description:

Updates the component's size by setting its inline and/or block dimensions.

Use this method to programmatically override the components's width (inline) and/or height (block).
Pass `null` to clear the override and revert to the default or CSS variable size.

members.name: displayMode
members.description:
Specifies the component's display mode, where:

`"dock"` displays at full height adjacent to center content,

`"overlay"` displays at full height on top of center content, and

`"float"` [Deprecated] does not display at full height with content separately detached from `calcite-action-bar` on top of center content.

`"float-content"` does not display at full height with content separately detached from `calcite-action-bar` on top of center content.

`"float-all"` detaches the `calcite-panel` and `calcite-action-bar` on top of center content.

Pass `null` to clear the override and revert to the default or CSS variable size.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, interaction is prevented and the component is displayed with lower opacity.

members.name: fillPlacement
members.description:
When `value` is specified for single values, determines the track's fill relative to the component's handle.

When `minValue` and `maxValue` are specified for multiple values, displays the fill between the `minValue` and `maxValue` handles.

members.name: form
members.description:
Specifies the `id` of the component's associated form.

When not set, the component is associated with its ancestor form element, if one exists.

members.name: groupSeparator
members.description: When `true`, number values are displayed with a group separator corresponding to the language and country format.

members.name: hasHistogram
members.description: When `true`, indicates a histogram is present.

members.name: histogram
members.description: Specifies a list of the histogram's x,y coordinates within the component's `min` and `max`. Displays above the component's track.

members.name: histogramStops
members.description: Specifies a list of single color stops for a histogram, sorted by offset in ascending order.

members.name: labelFormatter
members.description: When specified, allows users to customize handle labels.

type.text: (value: number, type: "value" | "min" | "max" | "tick", defaultFormatter: (value: number) => string) => string | undefined

members.name: labelHandles
members.description: When `true`, displays numeric value labels on handles.

members.name: labelText
members.description: Specifies the component's label text.

members.name: labelTicks
members.description: When `true` and `ticks` is specified, displays label tick marks with their numeric value.

members.name: max
members.description: The component's maximum selectable value.

members.name: maxLabel
members.description: When `minValue` and `maxValue` are specified for multiple values, specifies the accessible name for the `maxValue` handle, such as `"Temperature, upper bound"`.

members.name: maxValue
members.description: For multiple values, specifies the component's upper value.

members.name: messageOverrides
members.description: Overrides individual strings used by the component.

members.name: min
members.description: Specifies the component's minimum selectable value.

members.name: minLabel
members.description: Specifies the accessible name associated with the `value` handle (for single values) or `minValue` handle (for multiple values). For instance, `"Temperature, lower bound"`.

members.name: minValue
members.description: For multiple values, the component's lower value.

members.name: mirrored
members.description:
When `true`, the component will display values from high to low.

Note that this value will be ignored if the slider has an associated histogram.

members.name: name
members.description:
Specifies the name of the component.

Required to pass the component's `value` on form submission

members.name: numberingSystem
members.description: Specifies the Unicode numeral system used by the component for localization.

members.name: pageStep
members.description: Specifies the interval to move with the `Page up` or `Page down` keys.

members.name: precise
members.description: When `true`, sets a finer point for handles.

members.name: required
members.description:
When `true` and the component resides in a form,
the component must have a value in order for the form to submit.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the component's size.

members.name: snap
members.description: When `true` and `step` is specified, enables snap selection via a mouse.

members.name: status
members.description: Specifies the status of the input field, which determines message and icons.

members.name: step
members.description: Specifies the interval to move with the `Arrow up` or `Arrow down` keys.

members.name: ticks
members.description: Specifies the interval between tick marks on the number line.

members.name: validationIcon
members.description: Specifies the validation icon to display under the component.

members.name: validationMessage
members.description: Specifies the validation message to display under the component.

members.name: validity
members.description: The component's current validation state.

members.description: The component's value.

attributes.description: Specifies the Unicode numeral system used by the component for localization.

attributes.name: page-size
attributes.description: Specifies the page size of the component. When present, renders `calcite-pagination`.

attributes.name: scale
attributes.description: Specifies the component's size.

attributes.name: selection-display
attributes.description: When `selectionMode` is `"single"` or `"multiple"`, specifies the display of the selection interface. When  `selectionMode` is `"none"`, content slotted in the `selection-actions` slot will not be displayed.

attributes.name: selection-mode
attributes.description:
Specifies the selection mode of the component, where:

`"multiple"` allows any number of selections,

`"single"` allows only one selection, and

`"none"` does not allow any selections.

attributes.name: striped
attributes.description: When `true`, displays striped styling on the component's `calcite-table-rows`.

events.name: calciteTablePageChange
events.description: Emits when the component's page selection changes.

events.name: calciteTableSelect
events.description: Emits when the component's selected rows change.

slots.description: A slot for adding `calcite-table-row` elements containing `calcite-table-cell` and/or `calcite-table-header` elements.

slots.name: table-header
slots.description: A slot for adding `calcite-table-row` elements containing `calcite-table-header` elements.

slots.name: table-footer
slots.description: A slot for adding `calcite-table-row` elements containing `calcite-table-cell` and/or `calcite-table-header` elements.

slots.name: selection-actions
slots.description: A slot for adding `calcite-actions` or other elements to display when `selectionMode` is not `"none"` and `selectionDisplay` is not `"none"`.

members.name: layout
members.description:
Defines the layout of the component.

Use `"horizontal"` for rows, and `"vertical"` for a single column.

members.name: scale
members.description: Specifies the component's size.

members.name: selectedItems
members.description: Specifies the component's selected items.

members.name: selectionAppearance
members.description:
Specifies the selection appearance, where:

- `"icon"` (displays a checkmark or dot),
- `"highlight"` (changes the background color), and
- `"border"` (displays a border). [Deprecated] The `"border"` value is deprecated in v5.0.0, removal target v6.0.0 - Use `"highlight"` instead.

members.name: selectionMode
members.description:
Specifies the selection mode, where:

- `"multiple"` (allows any number of selected items),
- `"single"` (allows only one selected item),
- `"single-persist"` (allows only one selected item and prevents de-selection), and
- `"none"` (allows no selected items).

members.description: Closes the component when the `referenceElement` is clicked.

members.name: label
members.deprecated: in v1.5.0, removal target v6.0.0 - No longer necessary. Overrides the context of the component's text description, which could confuse assistive technology users.
members.description: Specifies an accessible label for the component.

members.name: offsetDistance
members.description: Specifies the distance to position the component away from the `referenceElement`.

members.name: offsetSkidding
members.description: Specifies the distance to position the component along the `referenceElement`.

members.name: open
members.description: When `true`, the component is open.

members.name: overlayPositioning
members.description:
Specifies the type of positioning to use for overlaid content, where:

`"absolute"` works for most cases - positioning the component inside of overflowing parent containers, which affects the container's layout, and

`"fixed"` is used to escape an overflowing parent container, or when the reference element's `position` CSS property is `"fixed"`.

members.name: placement
members.description: Determines where the component will be positioned relative to the `referenceElement`.

members.name: referenceElement
members.description:
The `referenceElement` is used to position the component according to its `placement` value.

Setting the value to an `HTMLElement` is preferred so the component does not need to query the DOM.

However, a string `id` of the reference element can also be used.

The component should not be placed within its own `referenceElement` to avoid unintended behavior.

members.name: topLayerDisabled
members.description:
When `true` and the component is `open`, disables top layer placement.

Only set this if you need complex z-index control or if top layer placement causes conflicts with third-party components.

members.description: Create a promise that resolves once component is fully loaded.

members.name: selectionMode
members.description:
Specifies the selection mode of the component, where:

`"ancestors"` displays with a checkbox and allows any number of selections from corresponding parent and child selections,

`"children"` allows any number of selections from one parent from corresponding parent and child selections,

`"multichildren"` allows any number of selections from corresponding parent and child selections,

`"multiple"` allows any number of selections,

`"none"` allows no selections,

`"single"` allows one selection, and

`"single-persist"` allows and requires one selection.

members.name: extraContainers
members.description: Additional elements to include in the focus trap. This is useful for including elements that may have related parts rendered outside the main focus-trap element.

members.name: setReturnFocus
members.description:
By default, when the focus trap is deactivated, focus will return to the element that was focused before the trap was activated. This option allows customizing that behavior.

Returning undefined will use the default behavior of returning focus to the element focused before activation.

members.name: hourFormat
members.description:
Specifies the component's hour format, where:

`"user"` displays the user's locale format,
`"12"` displays a 12-hour format, and
`"24"` displays a 24-hour format.

members.description: Translated messages and locale information

members.name: numberingSystem
members.description: Specifies the Unicode numeral system used by the component for localization.

members.name: step
members.description: Specifies the granularity the component's `value` must adhere to (in seconds).

members.name: value
members.description: The component's time value in ISO (24-hour) format.

members.name: calciteTimeChange
members.description: Change event that fires when the value is committed on Enter keypress or blur

members.name: fractionalSecond
members.description: The fractional second portion of the time value.

members.name: hour
members.description: The hour portion of the time value (in ISO 24-hour format).

members.name: hourFormat
members.description:
The effective hour format, calculated based on the component's hour-format property.
The component's hour-format of "user" will resolve to the locale's default hour format.

`"12"` displays a 12-hour format, and
`"24"` displays a 24-hour format.

members.name: locale
members.description: The language and region code to localize the time value.

members.name: localizedDecimalSeparator
members.description: The decimal separator used by the locale.

members.name: localizedFractionalSecond
members.description: The decimal separator used by the locale.

members.name: localizedHour
members.description: The localized fractional second portion of the time value.

members.name: localizedHourSuffix
members.description: The hour suffix used by the locale.

members.name: localizedMeridiem
members.description: The localized meridiem portion of the time value, when hourFormat resolves to 12.

members.name: localizedMinute
members.description: The localized minute portion of the time value.

members.name: localizedMinuteSuffix
members.description: The localized minute suffix used by the locale.

members.name: localizedSecond
members.description: The localized second portion of the time value.

members.name: localizedSecondSuffix
members.description: The localized second suffix used by the locale.

members.name: meridiem
members.description:
The effective meridiem of the time value, when hourFormat resolves to 12.

`"AM"` for hours between 0 and 12
`"PM"` for hours between 12 and 23

members.name: meridiemOrder
members.description: An integer representing the order in which the meridiem appears for the locale within an array of values returned by Intl.DateTimeFormat.

members.name: minute
members.description: The minute portion of the time value.

members.name: second
members.description: The second portion of the time value.

declarations.name: clearConfig
declarations.description:
Clears the effective config so it will be recomputed on next getConfig().

This is primarily intended for testing purposes.

members.name: focusTrapStack
members.description:
Defines the global trap stack to use for focus-trapping components.

This is useful if your application uses its own instance of `focus-trap` and both need to be aware of each other.

members.name: logLevel
members.description: Defines the global log level to use when logging messages.

declarations.name: Locale
declarations.description:
Represents any BCP 47 locale code used for formatting and parsing numbers.

For locales that are used for translations, please use the `SupportedLocale` type from `@arcgis/toolkit/intl`.
